subject: Chemistry
chapter: The d and f Block Elements

formulas.name: Empirical Formula
formulas.formula: Formula of simplest whole number ratio of atoms of each element in a compound
formulas.notes: Determined by calculating the simplest whole number ratio of atoms of each element

formulas.name: Molecular Formula
formulas.formula: Formula of actual whole number ratio of atoms of each element in a compound
formulas.notes: Determined by calculating the actual whole number ratio of atoms of each element

formulas.name: Empirical Formula Mass
formulas.formula: Empirical Formula Mass = (Atomic Mass of Element 1 × Number of Atoms of Element 1) + (Atomic Mass of Element 2 × Number of Atoms of Element 2) + ...
formulas.variables: Atomic Mass of Element, Number of Atoms of Element
formulas.notes: Used to determine the empirical formula of a compound

formulas.name: Molecular Formula Mass
formulas.formula: Molecular Formula Mass = (Atomic Mass of Element 1 × Number of Atoms of Element 1) + (Atomic Mass of Element 2 × Number of Atoms of Element 2) + ...
formulas.variables: Atomic Mass of Element, Number of Atoms of Element
formulas.notes: Used to determine the molecular formula of a compound

formulas.name: Percentage Yield
formulas.notes: Used to compare the actual yield of a reaction to the theoretical yield

formulas.name: Limiting Reagent
formulas.formula: Determine by calculating the amount of product obtained from each reactant, the reactant that produces the least amount of product is the limiting reagent
formulas.notes: Used to determine the limiting reagent in a chemical reaction

formulas.name: Electronic Configuration of d-block Elements
formulas.notes: Used to determine the electronic configuration of d-block elements

formulas.name: Electronic Configuration of f-block Elements
formulas.notes: Used to determine the electronic configuration of f-block elements
